title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Type-System Trace: Fault Injection Matrix
summary: Tests mock amqplib method calls but never assert routing or failure behavior; deployment starts consumers before topology migration; a reconnect storm follows broker restart; rollback cannot read new message schemas. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fault injection matrix increment in tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Fault Injection Matrix · Theory Fault Injection Matrix
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1802 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fault Injection Matrix contract for the supplied data.
